attempt: Move from bounded stateful certificates to global compactness pressure: finite decreasing quantities matter only if they can be upgraded into a theorem that rules out all infinite obstruction paths.
obstruction: A bounded tail state certificate still needs a compactness theorem controlling infinitely many zero contributions.
candidate_theorem: A global tail compactness theorem makes any off-critical zero produce a finite positivity failure.
next_experiment: Search admissible kernel families whose tail bounds are monotone and independently replayable.
claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.
